_comment: Showcase deck for the story-owned Scenario QA surface.

scenes.title: Scenario QA Story
scenes.subtitle: One scenario, deterministic transport legs, honest evidence, and Slidey reports
scenes.narration: Scenario QA turns the product-journey harness into a story-owned workflow that can preview, drive, judge, and report every supported transport.

scenes.title: Story Surface

items.label: Preview
items.detail: preview scenario=... transport=... expands deterministic scenario x transport legs without creating a run bundle.

items.label: Check
items.detail: check scenario=... transport=... creates the run bundle and drives one transport-pinned leg at a time.

items.label: Report
items.detail: report folds driver and independent judge outcomes into report.md and deck.slidey.json.

scenes.caption: The public surface is the Kitsoki story; lower-level Python remains deterministic implementation support.

scenes.title: Contracts

items.label: Schemas
items.detail: Versioned JSON Schemas define support data for persona, scenario, driver, run, leg result, transport-suite, and review shapes.

items.label: Completion state
items.detail: The internal adapter emits schemas/completion-state.schema.json so arena and CI consumers avoid stdout verdict scraping.

items.label: Transport axis
items.detail: TUI, web, VS Code bridge, and CLI legs carry explicit evidence contracts and proof levels.

scenes.caption: The story consumes machine-checkable contracts while keeping retained artifacts backward compatible.

scenes.title: No-LLM Gate

items.label: Flow tests
items.detail: kitsoki test flows stories/scenario-qa/app.yaml covers preview, run planning, leg pacing, and report close-out.

items.label: Runner tests
items.detail: transport_axis_test.py and scenario_qa_report_test.py cover transport expansion and Slidey deck folding without spend.

items.label: Story UI
items.detail: stories/scenario-qa calls the runner backend directly and keeps per-leg judgement independent.

scenes.caption: Automated gates stay deterministic; live capture remains an explicit operator-controlled workflow.
